          ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
          226. Bundle.entry:serviceRequest-follow-up-plan.response.modifierExtension
          Definition

          May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

          Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

          ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
          Comments

          There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

          Control0..*
          TypeExtension
          Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
          Summarytrue
          Requirements

          Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

          Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers

          ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
          228. Bundle.entry:serviceRequest-follow-up-plan.response.status
          Definition

          The status code returned by processing this entry. The status SHALL start with a 3 digit HTTP code (e.g. 404) and may contain the standard HTTP description associated with the status code.

          ShortStatus response code (text optional)
          Control1..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          230. Bundle.entry:serviceRequest-follow-up-plan.response.location
          Definition

          The location header created by processing this operation, populated if the operation returns a location.

          ShortThe location (if the operation returns a location)
          Control0..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          232. Bundle.entry:serviceRequest-follow-up-plan.response.etag
          Definition

          The Etag for the resource, if the operation for the entry produced a versioned resource (see Resource Metadata and Versioning and Managing Resource Contention).

          ShortThe Etag for the resource (if relevant)
          Comments

          Etags match the Resource.meta.versionId. The ETag has to match the version id in the header if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          234. Bundle.entry:serviceRequest-follow-up-plan.response.lastModified
          Definition

          The date/time that the resource was modified on the server.

          ShortServer's date time modified
          Comments

          This has to match the same time in the meta header (meta.lastUpdated) if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typeinstant
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          236. Bundle.entry:serviceRequest-follow-up-plan.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          238. Bundle.entry:encounters
          Slice Nameencounters
          Definition

          An entry in a bundle resource - will either contain a resource or information about a resource (transactions and history only).

          ShortEntry in the bundle - will have a resource or information
          Control1..*
          TypeBackboneElement
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsbdl-5: must be a resource unless there's a request or response (resource.exists() or request.exists() or response.exists())
          bdl-8: fullUrl cannot be a version specific reference (fullUrl.contains('/_history/').not())
          ele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          240. Bundle.entry:encounters.id

          Is Modifierfalse
          XML FormatIn the XML format, this property is represented as an attribute.
          Summaryfalse

          246. Bundle.entry:encounters.link
          Definition

          A series of links that provide context to this entry.

          ShortLinks related to this entry
          Control0..*
          TypeSee ttp://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Bundle#Bundle.link
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          248. Bundle.entry:encounters.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          250. Bundle.entry:encounters.resource
          Definition

          An interaction between a patient and healthcare provider(s) for the purpose of providing healthcare service(s) or assessing the health status of a patient.

          ShortAn interaction during which services are provided to the patient
          Control1..1
          TypeEncounter(Target Facility Encounter)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVisit

          260. Bundle.entry:encounters.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from SearchEntryMode
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/search-entry-mode|4.0.1)

          Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Typecode
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          262. Bundle.entry:encounters.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          Control0..1
          Typedecimal
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          264. Bundle.entry:encounters.request
          Definition

          Additional information about how this entry should be processed as part of a transaction or batch. For history, it shows how the entry was processed to create the version contained in the entry.

          ShortAdditional execution information (transaction/batch/history)
          Control0..1
          This element is affected by the following invariants: bdl-3
          TypeBackboneElement
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          272. Bundle.entry:encounters.request.method
          Definition

          In a transaction or batch, this is the HTTP action to be executed for this entry. In a history bundle, this indicates the HTTP action that occurred.

          ShortGET | HEAD | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH
          Control1..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from HTTPVerb
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/http-verb|4.0.1)

          HTTP verbs (in the HTTP command line). See HTTP rfc for details.

          Typecode
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          274. Bundle.entry:encounters.request.url
          Definition

          The URL for this entry, relative to the root (the address to which the request is posted).

          ShortURL for HTTP equivalent of this entry
          Comments

          E.g. for a Patient Create, the method would be "POST" and the URL would be "Patient". For a Patient Update, the method would be PUT and the URL would be "Patient/[id]".

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          Definition

          If the ETag values match, return a 304 Not Modified status. See the API documentation for "Conditional Read".

          Summarytrue

          300. Bundle.entry:encounters.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          312. Bundle.entry:risk-assessment-cardiovascular-disease.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          374. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.link
          Definition

          A series of links that provide context to this entry.

          ShortLinks related to this entry
          Control0..*
          TypeSee ttp://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Bundle#Bundle.link

          376. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          378. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.resource
          Definition

          Details and position information for a physical place where services are provided and resources and participants may be stored, found, contained, or accommodated.

          ShortDetails and position information for a physical place
          Control1..1
          TypeLocation(Providers Location)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse

          388. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from SearchEntryMode
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/search-entry-mode|4.0.1)

          Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Typecode
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          390. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          Control0..1
          Typedecimal
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          400. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.request.method
          Definition

          In a transaction or batch, this is the HTTP action to be executed for this entry. In a history bundle, this indicates the HTTP action that occurred.

          ShortGET | HEAD | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH
          Control1..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from HTTPVerb
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/http-verb|4.0.1)

          HTTP verbs (in the HTTP command line). See HTTP rfc for details.

          Typecode
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          402. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.request.url
          Definition

          The URL for this entry, relative to the root (the address to which the request is posted).

          ShortURL for HTTP equivalent of this entry
          Comments

          E.g. for a Patient Create, the method would be "POST" and the URL would be "Patient". For a Patient Update, the method would be PUT and the URL would be "Patient/[id]".

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension

          Definition

          Instruct the server not to perform the create if a specified resource already exists. For further information, see the API documentation for "Conditional Create". This is just the query portion of the URL - what follows the "?" (not including the "?").

          ShortFor conditional creates

          ShortStatus response code (text optional)

          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse

          Summarytrue

          Typeuri

          Summarytrue

          424. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.response.etag
          Definition

          The Etag for the resource, if the operation for the entry produced a versioned resource (see Resource Metadata and Versioning and Managing Resource Contention).

          ShortThe Etag for the resource (if relevant)
          Comments

          Etags match the Resource.meta.versionId. The ETag has to match the version id in the header if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          426. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.response.lastModified
          Definition

          The date/time that the resource was modified on the server.

          ShortServer's date time modified
          Comments

          This has to match the same time in the meta header (meta.lastUpdated) if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typeinstant
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          428. Bundle.entry:encounterLocations.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          reason why this should be supported.

          ShortEntry in the bundle - will have a resource or information

          TypeBackboneElement

          Must Supporttrue
          Summarytrue

          440. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          442. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.resource
          Definition

          Describes the intention of how one or more practitioners intend to deliver care for a particular patient, group or community for a period of time, possibly limited to care for a specific condition or set of conditions.

          ShortHealthcare plan for patient or group

          TypeCarePlan(Follow-up at HLC)
          Is Modifierfalse

          452. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from SearchEntryMode
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/search-entry-mode|4.0.1)

          Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Typecode
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          454. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          Control0..1
          Typedecimal
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          464. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.request.method
          Definition

          In a transaction or batch, this is the HTTP action to be executed for this entry. In a history bundle, this indicates the HTTP action that occurred.

          ShortGET | HEAD | POST | PUT | DELETE | PATCH
          Control1..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from HTTPVerb
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/http-verb|4.0.1)

          HTTP verbs (in the HTTP command line). See HTTP rfc for details.

          Typecode
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          466. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.request.url
          Definition

          The URL for this entry, relative to the root (the address to which the request is posted).

          ShortURL for HTTP equivalent of this entry
          Comments

          E.g. for a Patient Create, the method would be "POST" and the URL would be "Patient". For a Patient Update, the method would be PUT and the URL would be "Patient/[id]".

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          468. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.request.ifNoneMatch
          Definition

          If the ETag values match, return a 304 Not Modified status. See the API documentation for "Conditional Read".

          ShortFor managing cache currency
          Control0..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          470. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.request.ifModifiedSince
          Definition

          Only perform the operation if the last updated date matches. See the API documentation for "Conditional Read".

          ShortFor managing cache currency
          Control0..1
          Typeinstant
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          472. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.request.ifMatch
          Definition

          Only perform the operation if the Etag value matches. For more information, see the API section "Managing Resource Contention".

          ShortFor managing update contention
          Control0..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          474. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.request.ifNoneExist
          Definition

          Instruct the server not to perform the create if a specified resource already exists. For further information, see the API documentation for "Conditional Create". This is just the query portion of the URL - what follows the "?" (not including the "?").

          ShortFor conditional creates
          Control0..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          Definition

          ShortStatus response code (text optional)
          Control1..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          Definition

          ShortThe location (if the operation returns a location)
          Control0..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          488. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.response.etag
          Definition

          The Etag for the resource, if the operation for the entry produced a versioned resource (see Resource Metadata and Versioning and Managing Resource Contention).

          ShortThe Etag for the resource (if relevant)
          Comments

          Etags match the Resource.meta.versionId. The ETag has to match the version id in the header if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          490. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.response.lastModified
          Definition

          The date/time that the resource was modified on the server.

          ShortServer's date time modified
          Comments

          This has to match the same time in the meta header (meta.lastUpdated) if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typeinstant
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          492. Bundle.entry:careplan-hlc-follow-up.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          494. Bundle.entry:observation-physical-activity
          Slice Nameobservation-physical-activity
          Definition

          reason why this should be supported.

          ShortEntry in the bundle - will have a resource or information
          Control0..*
          TypeBackboneElement
          Is Modifierfalse
          Must Supporttrue
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsbdl-5: must be a resource unless there's a request or response (resource.exists() or request.exists() or response.exists())
          bdl-8: fullUrl cannot be a version specific reference (fullUrl.contains('/_history/').not())

          502. Bundle.entry:observation-physical-activity.link
          Definition

          A series of links that provide context to this entry.

          ShortLinks related to this entry
          Control0..*
          TypeSee ttp://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Bundle#Bundle.link
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          504. Bundle.entry:observation-physical-activity.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          506. Bundle.entry:observation-physical-activity.resource
          Definition

          Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject.

          ShortMeasurements and simple assertions
          Comments

          Used for simple observations such as device measurements, laboratory atomic results, vital signs, height, weight, smoking status, comments, etc. Other resources are used to provide context for observations such as laboratory reports, etc.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Physical Activity Status Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          518. Bundle.entry:observation-physical-activity.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          556. Bundle.entry:observation-physical-activity.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          568. Bundle.entry:observation-tobacco-smoker.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          570. Bundle.entry:observation-tobacco-smoker.resource
          Definition

          Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject.

          ShortMeasurements and simple assertions
          Comments

          Used for simple observations such as device measurements, laboratory atomic results, vital signs, height, weight, smoking status, comments, etc. Other resources are used to provide context for observations such as laboratory reports, etc.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Tobacco Smoker Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          632. Bundle.entry:observation-blood-pressure.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          634. Bundle.entry:observation-blood-pressure.resource
          Definition

          Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject.

          ShortMeasurements and simple assertions
          Comments

          Used for simple observations such as device measurements, laboratory atomic results, vital signs, height, weight, smoking status, comments, etc. Other resources are used to provide context for observations such as laboratory reports, etc.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Blood Pressure Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          684. Bundle.entry:observation-blood-pressure.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          696. Bundle.entry:observation-weight.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          698. Bundle.entry:observation-weight.resource
          Definition

          Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject.

          ShortMeasurements and simple assertions
          Comments

          Used for simple observations such as device measurements, laboratory atomic results, vital signs, height, weight, smoking status, comments, etc. Other resources are used to provide context for observations such as laboratory reports, etc.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Patient Weight Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          708. Bundle.entry:observation-weight.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from SearchEntryMode
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/search-entry-mode|4.0.1)

          Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Is Modifierfalse

          Summarytrue

          710. Bundle.entry:observation-weight.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          Control0..1
          Typedecimal
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension

          Definition

          The URL for this entry, relative to the root (the address to which the request is posted).

          ShortURL for HTTP equivalent of this entry
          Comments

          E.g. for a Patient Create, the method would be "POST" and the URL would be "Patient". For a Patient Update, the method would be PUT and the URL would be "Patient/[id]".

          746. Bundle.entry:observation-weight.response.lastModified
          Definition

          The date/time that the resource was modified on the server.

          ShortServer's date time modified
          Comments

          This has to match the same time in the meta header (meta.lastUpdated) if a resource is included.

          Control0..1
          Typeinstant

          748. Bundle.entry:observation-weight.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          Definition

          reason why this should be supported.

          ShortEntry in the bundle - will have a resource or information

          760. Bundle.entry:observation-height.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          762. Bundle.entry:observation-height.resource
          Definition

          Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject.

          ShortMeasurements and simple assertions
          Comments

          Used for simple observations such as device measurements, laboratory atomic results, vital signs, height, weight, smoking status, comments, etc. Other resources are used to provide context for observations such as laboratory reports, etc.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Patient Height Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          772. Bundle.entry:observation-height.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from SearchEntryMode
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/search-entry-mode|4.0.1)

          Why an entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          774. Bundle.entry:observation-height.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          Control0..1
          Typedecimal
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension

This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

          Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers

          ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
          822. Bundle.entry:observation-bmi.link
          Definition

          A series of links that provide context to this entry.

          ShortLinks related to this entry
          Control0..*
          TypeSee ttp://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Bundle#Bundle.link
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          824. Bundle.entry:observation-bmi.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          826. Bundle.entry:observation-bmi.resource
          Definition

          Measurements and simple assertions made about a patient, device or other subject.

          ShortMeasurements and simple assertions
          Comments

          Used for simple observations such as device measurements, laboratory atomic results, vital signs, height, weight, smoking status, comments, etc. Other resources are used to provide context for observations such as laboratory reports, etc.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Patient BMI Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          836. Bundle.entry:observation-bmi.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1
          BindingThe codes SHALL be taken from SearchEntryMode
          (required to http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/search-entry-mode|4.0.1)

          838. Bundle.entry:observation-bmi.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          ShortURL for HTTP equivalent of this entry

          E.g. for a Patient Create, the method would be "POST" and the URL would be "Patient". For a Patient Update, the method would be PUT and the URL would be "Patient/[id]".

          876. Bundle.entry:observation-bmi.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          888. Bundle.entry:observation-random-blood-sugar.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          890. Bundle.entry:observation-random-blood-sugar.resource
          Definition

          This observation may represent the result of a simple laboratory test such as hematocrit or it may group the set of results produced by a multi-test study or panel such as a complete blood count, a dynamic function test, a urine specimen study. In the latter case, the observation carries the overall conclusion of the study and references the atomic results of the study as "has-member" child observations

          ShortLaboratory result for a simple test or for a panel/study
          Comments

          Represents either a lab simple observation or the group of observations produced by a laboratory study.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Random Blood Sugar Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          952. Bundle.entry:observation-fasting-blood-sugar.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          954. Bundle.entry:observation-fasting-blood-sugar.resource
          Definition

          This observation may represent the result of a simple laboratory test such as hematocrit or it may group the set of results produced by a multi-test study or panel such as a complete blood count, a dynamic function test, a urine specimen study. In the latter case, the observation carries the overall conclusion of the study and references the atomic results of the study as "has-member" child observations

          ShortLaboratory result for a simple test or for a panel/study
          Comments

          Represents either a lab simple observation or the group of observations produced by a laboratory study.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Fasting Blood Sugar Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          1016. Bundle.entry:observation-cholesterol.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
          1018. Bundle.entry:observation-cholesterol.resource
          Definition

          This observation may represent the result of a simple laboratory test such as hematocrit or it may group the set of results produced by a multi-test study or panel such as a complete blood count, a dynamic function test, a urine specimen study. In the latter case, the observation carries the overall conclusion of the study and references the atomic results of the study as "has-member" child observations

          ShortLaboratory result for a simple test or for a panel/study
          Comments

          Represents either a lab simple observation or the group of observations produced by a laboratory study.

          Control1..1
          TypeObservation(Total Cholesterol Observation)
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summaryfalse
          Alternate NamesVital Signs, Measurement, Results, Tests

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          1030. Bundle.entry:observation-cholesterol.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          1068. Bundle.entry:observation-cholesterol.response.outcome
          Definition

          An OperationOutcome containing hints and warnings produced as part of processing this entry in a batch or transaction.

          ShortOperationOutcome with hints and warnings (for batch/transaction)
          Comments

          For a POST/PUT operation, this is the equivalent outcome that would be returned for prefer = operationoutcome - except that the resource is always returned whether or not the outcome is returned.

          This outcome is not used for error responses in batch/transaction, only for hints and warnings. In a batch operation, the error will be in Bundle.entry.response, and for transaction, there will be a single OperationOutcome instead of a bundle in the case of an error.

          Control0..1
          TypeResource
          Is Modifierfalse
          Summarytrue

          1080. Bundle.entry:practitioner.fullUrl
          Definition

          The Absolute URL for the resource. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except that:

          • fullUrl can be empty on a POST (although it does not need to when specifying a temporary id for reference in the bundle)
          • Results from operations might involve resources that are not identified.
          ShortURI for resource (Absolute URL server address or URI for UUID/OID)
          Comments

          fullUrl might not be unique in the context of a resource. Note that since FHIR resources do not need to be served through the FHIR API, the fullURL might be a URN or an absolute URL that does not end with the logical id of the resource (Resource.id). However, but if the fullUrl does look like a RESTful server URL (e.g. meets the regex, then the 'id' portion of the fullUrl SHALL end with the Resource.id.

          Note that the fullUrl is not the same as the canonical URL - it's an absolute url for an endpoint serving the resource (these will happen to have the same value on the canonical server for the resource with the canonical URL).

          Control1..1
          Typeuri
          Is Modifierfalse
          Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
          Summarytrue
          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))

          1092. Bundle.entry:practitioner.search.mode
          Definition

          Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

          Shortmatch | include | outcome - why this is in the result set
          Comments

          There is only one mode. In some corner cases, a resource may be included because it is both a match and an include. In these circumstances, 'match' takes precedence.

          Control0..1

          1094. Bundle.entry:practitioner.search.score
          Definition

          When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

          ShortSearch ranking (between 0 and 1)
          Comments

          Servers are not required to return a ranking score. 1 is most relevant, and 0 is least relevant. Often, search results are sorted by score, but the client may specify a different sort order.

          See Patient Match for the EMPI search which relates to this element.

          1122. Bundle.entry:practitioner.response.modifierExtension
          Definition

          May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

          Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

          ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
          Comments

          There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

          The status code returned by processing this entry. The status SHALL start with a 3 digit HTTP code (e.g. 404) and may contain the standard HTTP description associated with the status code.

          ShortStatus response code (text optional)

          Typestring

          1128. Bundle.entry:practitioner.response.etag
          Definition

          The Etag for the resource, if the operation for the entry produced a versioned resource (see Resource Metadata and Versioning and Managing Resource Contention).

          ShortThe Etag for the resource (if relevant)
          Comments

          Etags match the Resource.meta.versionId. The ETag has to match the version id in the header if a resource is included.

          Typestring
          Is Modifierfalse
